# Ant Code v1.3 Agent Extension Acceptance

Date: 2026-04-30

## Scope

This record closes Stage 6 through Stage 11 of
the internal TUI agent architecture upgrade plan.

## Stage Status

| Stage | Status | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stage 6 internal agents | Accepted | `/compact` uses the hidden `compaction` internal agent when the configured gateway is available and records model/fallback strategy metadata. |
| Stage 7 MCP runtime | Accepted | Persistent stdio runtime supports status, tools, prompts, resources, read-resource, reconnect, disconnect, stderr summaries, disabled servers, and Windows `npx` normalization. |
| Stage 8 skills | Accepted | Local skills support richer frontmatter, bounded reading, instruction-only runs, and `context: fork` execution through hidden child subagents. |
| Stage 9 background/worktree | Accepted | `/background` can run, list, show, cancel, and create/cleanup explicit git worktrees for isolated background tasks. |
| Stage 10 TUI base evaluation | Accepted | OpenTUI is documented as a deferred migration; current Ink TUI remains the production base. |
| Stage 11 tests/docs | Accepted | Targeted Stage 6-11 unit suite passed; full `npm run check` passed on 2026-04-30 with 260 tests. |

## Recommended Local MCP Entries

The root `lab-agent.config.json` and `config/lab-agent.lab-template.json`
include three disabled MCP entries:

- `filesystem`: local workspace file exploration/editing through the MCP
  protocol when the lab explicitly enables it.
- `memory`: local graph-style memory experiments for project facts and lab
  preferences.
- `playwright`: browser automation for local front-end verification.

All three are `enabled: false` by default. Enabling one requires an explicit
config edit and the normal Ant Code permission policy still evaluates tool
risk before execution. The high-sensitivity template intentionally leaves the
MCP list empty to avoid accidental `npx` package execution in restricted
projects.

## Local Skills

The checked-in `config/skills` directory contains three lab-owned skills:

- `codebase-orientation`: read-only project orientation and handoff recovery.
- `test-failure-triage`: fork-capable verifier workflow for failed tests or
  build commands.
- `release-readiness-review`: delivery review focused on tests, permissions,
  docs, rollback, and key leakage risk.

Skills remain local files. Ant Code does not fetch remote skill marketplace
content, does not execute scripts inside skill folders, and does not route
skills to provider-owned backends.

## Manual Acceptance Already Reported

- Long-answer scrolling works in large and small windows.
- `/resume`, `/sessions`, and `/guide` work in the live TUI.
- Two-press `Esc` interrupt works.
- Slash command help and visible `Esc` exit guidance were accepted.
- `/compact` is callable and uses model summarization when the gateway is
  available, with local fallback otherwise.

## Remaining Release Gate

Latest verification:

```powershell
npm run check
```

Result on 2026-04-30: passed. The run covered syntax, forbidden endpoint scan,
provenance records, dependency policy, and 260 unit/integration tests.

For a model-enabled rollout, the lab operator should also run one live gateway
smoke test in the target environment and confirm no gateway key is written to
repository files.
